items.speaker: Rover
items.text: "The midnight chime has faded, yet the night endures. Restless hearts long for an understanding that should be ours."

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: "In the freest place of Ragunna, I await your arrival..." This should be the place Capollo mentioned in his invitation.

items.speaker: Invitation Letter
items.text: "With this invitation, I wish to personally thank you for all you have done for the Montelli family and to amend the slight misstep in your reception. I hope this gesture will help to mend any misunderstandings and strengthen our alliance."

items.speaker: Invitation Letter
items.text: "In the name of the Montellis, I shall honor my allies as I do my own kin and blood."

items.speaker: Invitation Letter
items.text: "In the freest place of Ragunna, I await your arrival... Yours sincerely, Capollo Montelli."

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: I knew it, another invitation. That's kind of their signature, huh? But this wasn't Carlotta, who's this <color=yellow>Capollo</color> guy?

options.text: He's the uncle Carlotta told us about.
He's the owner of that pendant.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Oh, yeah. The big boss of the vault and all that jazz? Guess it won't hurt swinging by Carlotta's family. They do owe us a big one!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: I remember now, didn't he get nabbed by the Order?

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Then he's got a lot to thank you for. When that sneaky Priest tried to use him as a pawn, things could've gotten messy without you and Carlotta's quick thinking.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: I bet he's got something good planned. A pile of money? Maybe a mega feast?

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Ugh... hic! I've been feeling pretty weird lately, my head's all wobbly. I might not be able to show right when you need me!

options.text: Just get some rest, I can manage.
I'll make sure to eat all the food for you.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Okay, you've got this. If anything happens, just smash your way through!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Alright, alright! No need to rub it in!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Anyway, I'll leave this to you then. But hey, if you ever need my help, just give me a shout, okay?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Hm, to locate me so precisely and deliver the invitation right into my hands... this Capollo Montelli knows what he's doing.
Forgive me for not meeting you properly until now.
Guest from the Black Shores, Laureate of the Carnevale, savior and friend of the Montellis... {PlayerName}, your presence is an honor.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: Allow me to formally introduce myself. I am Capollo Montelli, Carlotta's uncle, <color=yellow>son of the Padrino</color>, and <color=yellow>the next head of the family</color>.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (Given Capollo's stature, a private meeting at this hour... is it really just about gratitude?)

options.text: Why the sudden invite from someone of your rank?
I hope this meeting is as simple as it seems.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: Of course, just as stated in the invitation. I'm a man of my word.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: Without you, the traitors wouldn't have been captured, and I freed from their hands.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: While Carlotta has expressed our family's gratitude, I feel obliged to do it in person.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: But unfortunately, the traitors had little to offer, and the one behind them is still in the shadows. Unnerving, isn't it?

options.text: Don't worry, Carlotta is on it.
I heard it's someone high up in the family.
Sorry, your family issues aren't my concern.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: I'll wait for her findings then.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: Hmm... that does narrow down the possibilities.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: Be as involved as you like, {PlayerName}. I won't think any less of you.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: Things aren't as bright for the Montellis as they may seem. The Order, Fisalias, and the Fractsidus... Any attempt to break the status quo puts us at risk.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: The traitors we captured were a warning, but also a reminder—to survive, I must seek alliances beyond the family.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: Especially with you, just as Carlotta did.

options.text: You or Carlotta, what's the difference?
Thought I was already an ally to the entire family.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: Now that you mention it, let me be clear.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: Carlotta is dear to me, and nothing will change that.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: But she's from the lesser branch of the family. Tradition says I, the rightful heir, should manage dealings with the Black Shores, not her.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: Restoring order and benefiting the family as I intended... that's what I meant in the letter by amending a slight misstep.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: The leader of the Black Shores must have sent you here with a purpose. Tell me, and I will offer you support far beyond what Carlotta could provide.

options.text: I can't give you an answer now.
Support with a high price, I suppose.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: The choice is yours, of course... But as the family heir, I will not see my authority undermined, even by those I consider allies.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: So if you can't stand by my side, I'd prefer you stay out of the Montellis' affairs.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: Otherwise... believe me, you won't want to find yourself at odds with us, no matter how strong you are.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: You're welcome to remain an observer from the sidelines. But once you join the table, you play by the rules—my rules.
Well said. But the rules are not set in stone, are they?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: What matter of intrigue brought you and {PlayerName} together at this hour, Uncle? A table... are we speaking of a splendid card game, then?

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: Your timing is most unfortunate. Our little chat just finished.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Pity. I didn't think you'd pull {Male=him;Female=her} aside for a private word, especially after all Rover and I have been through together.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: But since you've finished, would you excuse us now?

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: Carlotta, you stay. I have more to discuss with you.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Meet me at the <color=yellow>rendezvous</color>. Beware the "eyes."

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: A gun is a powerful tool, but only in the hands of the worthy.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: I understand, Uncle. I've never forgotten where my privileges come from, nor will I covet what's not mine.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: The Black Shores, the Carnevale... my sole principle is whether they benefit the Montellis.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: I will execute any command that strengthens the family, and crush any threats to our standing.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: ...Very well. Keep your words in mind.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Uncle, about Benett... have you heard?

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: That family thief stealing from the vault? Why, are you suggesting I failed my duties?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Not at all, I just happened to overhear someone talking about it in passing...

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (Carlotta's not here yet. Guess I'll have to wait.)

items.speaker: Old Gentleman
items.text: Might I trouble you for a light, young {Male=man;Female=lady}?

options.text: Sorry, I don't have one.

items.speaker: Old Gentleman
items.text: Ah well, a cigar without smoke... My granddaughter told me Ragunna's rain is a bad omen. Can't argue with that now, can I?

options.text: (Is he here by accident or design?)
(I can sound him out a bit.)

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: You've been looking at this painting for a while. Anything special about it?

items.speaker: Old Gentleman
items.text: Oh, did it capture your interest as well? Or are you simply passing the time until the rain ends?

options.text: Just happened to see you studying the painting.
You could say I'm waiting.

items.speaker: Old Gentleman
items.text: Haha, it's only the investor's nature in me. I find myself irresistibly drawn to items of unique value.

options.text: (So he's an art investor...)

items.speaker: Old Gentleman
items.text: Then it seems you will have to put up with me for a while.

options.text: (So he's waiting out the rain...)

items.speaker: Old Gentleman
items.text: The Montelli family prides themselves on their eclectic taste in art. Any style by any artist can find its home within their quarter.

items.speaker: Old Gentleman
items.text: Art knows no boundaries of time, place, or the mind. Here we stand, united by the rain, drawn to the same painting.

items.speaker: Old Gentleman
items.text: They say in art, we glimpse pieces of our own soul.

items.speaker: Old Gentleman
items.text: What do you make of this piece? Care to share your thoughts?

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: You step closer to study the collage.

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: Small, irregular shapes nest within larger circles, their edges melding together to form a delicate harmony.

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: This balance, however, feels precarious, as if two opposing forces are on the verge of collision.

options.text: The large circle completes itself by absorbing the small pieces.
It's too abstract for me.
Maybe the artist is seeking some kind of change.

items.speaker: Old Gentleman
items.text: You have keener eyes than I expected. Seeing essence beneath the surface.

items.speaker: Old Gentleman
items.text: Hahaha, it is typical of symbolism to conceal ideas in a play of colors and forms... But sometimes, simplicity is more powerful.

items.speaker: Old Gentleman
items.text: Indeed. True artistry lies not only in what is painted, but in what is left unsaid.

items.speaker: Old Gentleman
items.text: I share the same view, except that I'm somehow saddened by the part that forsakes its character to achieve balance.

items.speaker: Old Gentleman
items.text: ...Perhaps chosen by the artist for its uniqueness.

items.speaker: Old Gentleman
items.text: Now is not the time to cling to the existing balance, but to embrace the unknown, seek change, and carve out one's path... just as she once did.

options.text: Seek change? You mean this painting?

items.text: As the rain finally ceases, the elderly man who had been admiring the painting quietly departs, leaving you alone to contemplate the painting's deeper meaning.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: "Glory in Decay," the impressionist Pasoli's latest piece... Didn't know you had such refined taste.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: I dodged those pesky "eyes" the whole way here, worried you'd been kept waiting too long. But it seems you've kept yourself entertained.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: So the old gentleman was simply hiding from the rain, was he? I see... The Montelli Quarter does attract art enthusiasts. Anyway, I'm glad prying eyes didn't follow.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: When it rains in Ragunna, surprises are as common as puddles. A delay is only a small splash.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Calling you here with little explanation and a word of caution... I can only imagine your confusion.

options.text: It's taken me by surprise.
You and your uncle both...
The Montellis enjoy their intrigue, don't they?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: The Montelli's path is not always paved with roses, but with thorns as well. As the heir apparent, my uncle bears many burdens.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Seeing me work with you on the Carnevale under Grandfather's orders might have unsettled him, leading to a hasty, misguided move.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: In truth, I haven't thought about it, and my opinions hold little weight.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: The current plan has proven effective. I believe it's wise to trust Grandfather's judgment, as I always do.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Besides, I now have more important duties to fulfill.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: The shark in our midst has finally made its move.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Recently, several of our partners claimed to have found a superior seller and ended their dealings with us.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Meanwhile, Benett, our vault keeper, was reported to have embezzled and sold vault assets. It's said that he tried to flee but was killed by Tacet Discords outside the city.

options.text: So Benett betrayed the family as well?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: I am not so sure... I've worked with Benett before. He was principled, bold but not reckless, not one to easily betray. And...

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: The night he died, he sent a photo to my Terminal—a blood-stained page of an anthology, marked with three phrases. "Thy eyes guide my course in the tempest," "When forty winters siege thy beauty," and "The planet turns right, twice and a half."

options.text: That's rather cryptic.
Okay, but... it makes no sense.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: The meaning is yet to be revealed, but he is clearly conveying a message.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: All evidence points to Benett being the key to uncovering the shark lurking in our waters.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: The Montelli family is a symbol of liberation for Ragunna. We uphold freedom and equality for all. Our vault is the heart of our family, and if it's corrupted, it threatens not only us, but risks plunging the city back into oppression and isolation.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: As much as I hate to suspect family members, I cannot rule any of them out.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: You, however, are different. As an outsider who has aided us in capturing the initial traitors... I hope to not be proven wrong, but I feel that I can trust you.

options.text: So you're asking for my help.
I suppose this is when I volunteer to help.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: My uncle's words were clear. Only those at the table can play the game. To engage with my family, one must have both standing and purpose.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: So, I offer you a new identity in our upcoming inquiry—Cat's Eye, a provisional member under my patronage.

options.text: But I'm not truly a member of the family.
This... is obviously a lie.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: If you wish to join, you must undertake the final assessment and pledge your loyalty to the Montellis.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Oh, don't worry, we aren't harming anyone. It is but a subtle adaptation of the rules.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: When it comes to family matters, we like to use codenames, each tied to a unique token. Mine, for example, is Opal.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Opal recommended Cat's Eye. It has a ring to it, don't you think?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Also, have you looked closely at your own eyes? They share the same luster as Cat's Eye gems.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Now, Cat's Eye, will you stand with me to catch the traitor within our family and avert this looming threat?

options.text: Well, you recommended me, so it's your call.
We must prevent this threat and bring peace to Ragunna.
I want to uncover the true identity of this lurking shark.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: I understand decorum is important to the Montellis. But among friends, can't we be a bit more... informal?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: What made you say that?

options.text: You're always one step ahead. How about we slow down and savor the present?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Hmm, you're curious who I am beyond my role, is that it?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Hmm, I just might entertain that thought.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: But for now, my agents just sent me the address of Benett's residence. While his body has been found, what he left behind is still out there.

options.text: Let's try our luck at his residence.

dialog.stepLabel: 前往贝内特在莫塔里区的住所
dialog.nodeDesc: 前往贝内特在莫塔里区的住所

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: It seems someone has acted faster. Those two... They're my uncle's agents, Sage and Acorn.

options.text: Codenames?
Those are not real names.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Yes, we address each other by codenames—handy identifiers in a crowd.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: I'll keep you updated on the family members' codenames we encounter, along with any other details you might need.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Seems like they are looking for something as well.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: It's only natural my uncle wishes to retrieve the lost items... but my presence might raise a few eyebrows.

options.text: I'll go have a little chat with them to see what they know.
You can find a way around them and continue the investigation.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Yes, you try and loosen their lips. If memory serves, they are senior members, having served under my uncle for years. Regulars at Margherita's.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Talk business with merchants, and wine with drinkers... Engage their interests, and their words will flow like a river. As for the rest...

options.text: I'll improvise as needed.

items.text: You rendezvous with Carlotta after her investigation and share the information you gathered.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: So, they found nothing, but assumed Uncle wasn't concerned?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: That makes no sense. Everything that happened recently ties back to the vault. If Uncle wished to protect his position, he'd be quick to retrieve the items and resolve the situation.

options.text: Maybe he's hiding something.
Maybe he's in control all along.
Things don't add up.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Curiously, Benett was last sighted in the vicinity of <color=yellow>Liberty Square</color>, where you met Uncle.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Last night, I was investigating the area before I happened upon your private meeting.

options.text: That explains why you were there...

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: But when I brought this up to him afterward, he didn't seem privy to any details.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: As for my search of Benett's home, nothing of note turned up, just as the two agents reported.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: I found no spoils, not even the faintest trace of them being stored or moved... Everything about his living space fits the profile of a typical junior vault employee.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: I've also asked around about Bennet after our previous collaboration. He was ambitious, but never greedy. I was even told he refused the bribe offered by a business associate during an operation.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: The Montelli family is ever generous with wealth and opportunities, welcoming all who aspire to make their mark in Ragunna. Mere profit would never make him betray us.

options.text: The family protects its members.
Members bring the family glory.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: For now, more evidence is required. The entire matter might need to be approached from a different angle...

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Shh, hold still... See that man over there, the one that looks like a gray dove?

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: Without making a movement, you follow Carlotta's line of sight. Not far away, a man, partially hidden, is frequently glancing in your direction. He seems to be observing you and Carlotta.

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: Noticing your gaze, he immediately starts running.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: After him, quick.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Nowhere to run. Now it's time for a proper chat.
Hah... These old, creaky bones of mine.
Did I offend you? Or are you just chasing down every passing soul... Never thought the Montellis would grow so aggressive.

options.text: No running, no chasing.
Just a glance and you can tell we're Montellis?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: He had no choice but to run, to escape his past and sever any ties with the Montellis.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: No one knows the family's ways better than its members, even though... he is no longer one of us.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Right, Old Duncan?

items.speaker: Duncan
items.text: Ha, hahaha... I must admit, you have an annoyingly good memory.

items.speaker: Duncan
items.text: It's remarkable that someone of your rank would remember a man such as myself.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: I will never forget that <color=yellow>stormy</color> night, nor any of the members who were caught in it.

items.speaker: Duncan
items.text: Sadly, I've no desire to be remembered. To me, Montelli is only a name of the past.

items.speaker: Duncan
items.text: There were moments of joy, but they cannot make up for my loss... now I only feel tired.

options.text: If that's true, why are you here?
You say one thing, but do another.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Why go back to what you claim to have left in the past? Unless there's something you can't ignore.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: You were standing across from Benett's doorway, and your clothes are still damp from the rain... You weren't simply passing by, you were watching. What were you looking for? Or should I ask, what did you hope to find?

items.speaker: Duncan
items.text: I don't know.

items.speaker: Duncan
items.text: To grieve for my last friend in the family? To blame him for being so reckless and greedy?

items.speaker: Duncan
items.text: Or to figure out how this fool ended up in such a mess, hoping to find the lost items and settling his debts, so he may rest in peace?

items.speaker: Duncan
items.text: I tried to persuade him to leave with me, but failed. In Ragunna's game, the Montelli family has only two options.

items.speaker: Duncan
items.text: We either play to win, or are forced to leave with nothing.

items.speaker: Duncan
items.text: Every Montelli has a chance at their desires, but what's the price... They stake everything they hold dear—family, love, faith, principles, and even their life.

items.speaker: Duncan
items.text: Now... his ambition and greed have cost him everything.

options.text: But you can decide when to leave the table.
Is that why you left the family?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Most people find acceptance easier than change, as change demands the bravery to face uncertainty. No great endeavor is without its perils.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: So yes, if the Montellis are to drive the change and push past current barriers, the path ahead will be anything but easy.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: The family exists to withstand what an individual cannot, and accomplish what might otherwise be unattainable.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: But within the family, each Montelli still enjoys the freedom of choice. Your leaving is a testiment to this, is it not?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: If you're willing, there is still much to be done for Benett. His Terminal, the alleged stolen goods... everything but his body remains missing.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Given your familiarity, you might help us in locating these items...

options.text: Benett's death may not be as simple as it looks.
We were also looking for more clues in his home.

items.speaker: Duncan
items.text: ...In that case, you might want to speak to Scarlet Velvet.

options.text: Who is this Scarlet Velvet?

items.speaker: Duncan
items.text: The leader of a network of commoners seeking freedom in Ragunna City. They had close ties with Benett. If he was really stealing, I'd wager they were complicit.

items.speaker: Duncan
items.text: That said, with their recent fallout with the family, I can't guarantee you'll find them, let alone talk to them.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: We have little choice but to try.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Now... let's go, {PlayerName}. We're off to the most reliable source of information in Ragunna.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: This is the place.

options.text: The best place for information... is a pub?
I don't think Scarlet Velvet would hide somewhere so public.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: She may not be here, but I have my ways of reaching her people.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: In Ragunna's market, they say fortune seldom favors those who lack a taste for fine drinks.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Among traders, there's a secret <color=yellow>"code"</color> in the way drinks are mixed.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: A base of bitter almond and mint, for instance, usually signals danger and death. But some have devised their own, more private codes.

options.text: So they send messages by what they order.
So it's not just a drink, it's a conversation.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Yes. This area is essentially a neutral zone in the city. No affiliations, no restrictions.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: With the ongoing feud between Scarlet Velvet and the Montellis, finding her will be difficult. But I've heard they have eyes and ears here.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: So all that remains is to order the right drink, watch the crowd, and let their reactions lead us to Scarlet Velvet's contact.

items.speaker: Bartender
items.text: Greetings, what will it be today?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: A Scarlet Velvet Ribbon, please.

items.speaker: Bartender
items.text: Scarlet Velvet... An uncommon choice. Perhaps the one you seek is waiting just as patiently. May you both find what you've come for.

items.speaker: Cold Acolyte
items.text: Terminals are divine artifacts from the Sentinel, brought to Rinascita by the first Primus.

items.speaker: Cold Acolyte
items.text: What you possessed were nothing but privately made replicas, existing solely by the grace of the Primus. How dare you defy our cleansing of such blasphemy!

items.speaker: Scarlet Velvet
items.text: We never stopped asking the Order for permission to wear the Terminals, but every single request is denied. You won't even tell us why!

items.speaker: Scarlet Velvet
items.text: You've controlled the Terminal supply, and the family stopped trading with us... These are all we have left. Why should we hand them over?

items.speaker: Cold Acolyte
items.text: I can sense your fervent desire for the artifacts and the blessings from the Sentinel... But whether you are worthy of the terminals is for the Order to decide.

items.speaker: Cold Acolyte
items.text: If you willingly part with your wealth in devotion to the Order's divine cause, the door to forgiveness might still be open.

items.speaker: Cold Acolyte
items.text: But should you persist in your defiance... we'll have to escort you to the Penitentiary for judgment.

options.text: People are quarreling there.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: The Order and the commoners... it must be Scarlet Velvet causing the stir.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: The Order's arrogance is known, but such blatant actions... Is it because we're far from the heart of the city?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Let's first address their troubles, and then we'll have a proper talk.

items.speaker: Cold Acolyte
items.text: Ah, the Montelli's second daughter and this year's Laureate. I suggest you tread carefully and avoid crossing the Order and the Sentinel, especially not for the sake of mere commoners.

items.speaker: Cold Acolyte
items.text: Must I remind you again? Trading Reserved Terminals is a breach of the discipline.

options.text: I didn't see anyone trading Terminals here.
All I see is you taking what's rightfully theirs.

items.speaker: Scarlet Velvet
items.text: We paid good money for these. If you're taking them, you owe us a refund.

items.speaker: Cold Acolyte
items.text: Yet the very <color=yellow>traders</color> who took your money have seen the error of their ways and reported your dealings. They have sworn to repent for their illicit production and cease all trade with you.

options.text: (Illicit production, traders... Is he talking about the Montellis?)

items.speaker: Cold Acolyte
items.text: How sad, Ms. Carlotta. Such loyalty to your family, only to be left in the dark. No one bothered to inform you, did they?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Oh, please. My ties with the family are not so fragile as to be shaken by mere words.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: The fact that we are having this conversation at all means that you cannot ignore my status, wouldn't you agree?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: The Sentinel and the Primus have declared free trade as our undeniable right. Scarlet Velvet is always entitled to trade as they please, regardless of their previous partnerships.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: You preach the doctrine, yet fail to adhere to it yourself. If I were you, I'd be on my way to the Penitentiary. Willful transgression... is the worst blasphemy one can imagine.

items.speaker: Cold Acolyte
items.text: Mark my words! The day Montelli is brought to its knees by the Order and Fisalia will be the day you beg for the Sentinel's mercy.

items.speaker: Cold Acolyte
items.text: When that day comes, all shall be under the Order's control. And it will not be long in coming.
The Acolyte departs with his entourage. Scarlet Velvet signals the others to leave and assess the damages.

items.speaker: Scarlet Velvet
items.text: Ernesto, the bartender, said you needed to see me.

items.speaker: Scarlet Velvet
items.text: You may have just defended us, but you heard it—your family has left us in a mess. Frankly, I don't see the point in having this conversation.

items.speaker: Scarlet Velvet
items.text: But since Ernesto said it's about Benett, I'll give you a minute, for his sake. Speak quickly.

options.text: What you heard could be another lie from the Order.
What if the Order is trying to turn us against each other?

items.speaker: Scarlet Velvet
items.text: Regardless of the bigger picture, your side ended the deal. We were informed on short notice to stop all trade.

items.speaker: Scarlet Velvet
items.text: And it's not just the Terminals—no more "jewelry," "perfumes," and all that nonsense. You saw what happened today.

items.speaker: Scarlet Velvet
items.text: Not to mention I haven't seen a Montelli for nearly two weeks.

items.speaker: Scarlet Velvet
items.text: Where were you when we were fighting against the Order and the Fisalias?

options.text: Montelli has been dealing with its own troubles lately.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: There was a slight issue of a traitor in the vault, so our hands were tied.

options.text: You sure don't beat around the bush.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: A direct approach works best at times. Actually, what that Acolyte claimed is news to me. Surrendering control and ceasing trade... are not in the Montelli's vocabulary.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: The Montelli creed has always been family first, and a pioneering spirit second.

items.speaker: Scarlet Velvet
items.text: With your grandfather, maybe, but he won't be around forever. When Capollo takes the helm... you won't have a say.

items.speaker: Scarlet Velvet
items.text: The real question is whether we can get our trade back on track. Can your family still be trusted? What I need is a solid promise from someone in charge—can you do that?

items.speaker: Scarlet Velvet
items.text: One thing's for sure, I don't want to see the family who first broke the monopoly to trade with us commoners end up defending it.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: I'll speak with my uncle and grandfather to see what happened with our cooperation.

items.speaker: Scarlet Velvet
items.text: You know what? This is supposed to be your family's business. Why am I saying this to you... Excuse my earlier words.

items.speaker: Scarlet Velvet
items.text: So... Ernesto told me Benett's gone. What happened? He seemed fine the last time we met.

items.speaker: Scarlet Velvet
items.text: Good judgment there! The rumors are nonsense… Fencing stolen goods? Please. Benett used to handle our shipments for the vault, but everything was above board.

items.speaker: Scarlet Velvet
items.text: Nothing shady, okay? He knew his stuff—never took advantage of us, never budged on prices either.

items.speaker: Scarlet Velvet
items.text: For him, it was always business first, friendship second. When he was running late, he'd stay up that <color=yellow>hill</color> sometimes. We even set up a shelter there after it happened a few times. That's all.

items.speaker: Scarlet Velvet
items.text: Come to think of it, he did stay there for a while the last time we saw him. Asked us to stash something too. What was it he said… "Hope we don't need to use it."

options.text: (So Benett left something behind on purpose...)

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: It seems Benett may have anticipated the trouble ahead.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: I wonder if you might show us to the shelter? We'd be most grateful.

items.speaker: Scarlet Velvet
items.text: There's <color=yellow>an entrance along the coast</color>. Go straight down the road and you will see it. What's your business there, anyway?

options.text: To test our hypothesis and see if there's anything of importance hidden.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: You can tell someone was here, look at the mess. Perhaps Benett deliberately arranged it this way as a cover.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: We'll each use our sensors to search, then let's gather and analyze the clues we find.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Someone extracted the core data module... It's empty now.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: This must be what Benett was trying to tell us before he died. Someone with access to the vault has broken their oath and leaked the data module. Now, only Uncle, Grandfather, and I have the clearance to retrieve it.

options.text: So that clears up all the questions.
I see. But what can he gain from it?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: In Rinascita, the Order maintains control over Terminals, unlike other countries where they are freely traded. My grandfather learned to modify them during his early ventures in the New Federation.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Our lantern-type Terminal is a modified model. It may look purely ornamental, but it houses the tech modules we've developed. As Scarlet Velvet said, aside from our art dealings, we've been supplying these Terminals to Resonators at fair prices as part of our discreet business.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: By doing so, we've broken the Order's monopoly. We've given more people the means to protect themselves in their ventures, giving them choices they didn't have before.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: The Order is aware of our discreet business, but they turn a blind eye as long as we don't openly challenge their authority. The hefty taxes we pay maintain this... delicate understanding.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: As a result, no family member would ever divulge our Terminal secrets, much less allow the modules to fall into the wrong hands. What exactly are you planning, Uncle?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: No, we must find the core module...

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: Carlotta, where are you?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Uncle, may I ask you something first? Before the Carnevale, the Priest showed me your pendant. How did he come by it, and what led to your capture?

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: Does it matter? All you need to know is that it's handled, and I'm in the clear now.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Were you mistreated? Did they coerce you into doing something against your will?

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: Heed my words. Whatever you've uncovered, put it behind you, and all will be as it was. No grudges, no consequences, only family.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: Now, we've had some trouble with a shipment over at Septimont. As the rightful heir, I'm giving you a new order. Take care of it, and I'll pretend none of this ever happened.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: I refuse.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: Excuse me?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: I said I refuse! I can't act as if nothing happened. I won't let a family member die unjustly, or see our modules fall into the wrong hands.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: Don't trouble yourself with the module. Yes, our family's ambition has led us far, but it's exacted a price paid in blood and tears. What harm is there in choosing the safer, easier path?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: I can't believe you just said that. So, you're refusing to tell the truth and reveal the module's location, is that it?

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: Why do you insist on tearing everything apart? Have you ever thought about what Padrino would say? What others will think of us? I've given you an out, all you need to do is accept it.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Then so be it.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Your course is set. As is mine. But mark my words... I will stop you, Mr. Capollo.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: I'm sorry you have to see this side of our family.

options.text: It's okay to be a little less perfect with friends.
It's messy, but still manageable.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Really? I just wish... No, I did promise I'd try to drop the pretense with you.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: I may need more time to come to terms with all this. I don't regret uncovering the truth and exposing the traitor… I'm just saddened that my uncle is the one behind it all.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: We were meant to be the guardians of our family's ideals, to carry the torch forward. And yet, now…

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Well, wallowing in emotion won't solve anything. Our priority now is to recover the data module. Every moment it's missing puts our family at greater risk.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: The core module is difficult to carry around. They must have hidden it... The dock Benett visited, used for storage... Could it be there?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: We need to adjust our strategy. My conversation with Capollo will have alerted him. He's likely to move the module sooner than expected. I need to inspect the dock for any clues.

options.text: We may restore more data in this Terminal.
There could still be other possibilities.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Indeed, but time is not on our side. If Capollo acts on this, I might lose my window to secure the module. Our sole advantage now is that we're operating as a pair.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: I need you to take this terminal to <color=yellow>Pierre</color> at the bank. If the dock turns out to be a dead end, we will need other leads.

options.text: What's your next move if we find the module?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: I know that securing the module alone won't suffice. I must uncover Capollo's true motives...how it will affect our family. I'll figure it out, I promise.
According to the plan, you and Carlotta part ways. She heads alone to the dock she mentioned.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: (Nothing so far... Was I wrong? Maybe this place doesn't hold what we were looking for? Why did Benett come here, then?)

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: (Or am I too late... Did Capollo get here first?)

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: (And the energy fluctuation... it was going off and on... No, I couldn't have been imagining it.)

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: (Such a big mirror... Why is it here?)

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: (It doesn't seem to be made of common glass, but some kind of crystallized energy... Oh.)

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: (Will I find the data module on the other side...)

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: (Or was all this just a trap...)
You arrive at the bank as Carlotta said, looking for the expert in Terminal studies named Pierre.
You tell Carlotta what you learned from the recovered Terminal data.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: I see... so Duncan was in the dark about the real cause of Benett's death. He simply wanted to protect his friend's reputation, and Capollo used that.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Duncan gave us a rigged Terminal to cover Benett's "debt", clearing his name.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Capollo <color=yellow>couldn't have tampered</color> with the Terminal data himself, or he would have found the information hidden beneath the overwrites. He simply laid a trap at <color=yellow>the dock that Benett visited</color>.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: I can empathize with Duncan, even to some extent Capollo. They are driven by a desire for safety and stability—a tempting future even for me.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: But we will never achieve that by surrendering ourselves to the Order.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: From the very beginning, our paths diverged. To yield to them now means giving up even more, and once we lose our grip, we'll no longer have the luxury of refusal.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Grandfather and the others have already paved the way. I will follow their lead and chart a course towards a brighter tomorrow. For our family, and all of Ragunna.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Wealth and power will safeguard our freedom, while freedom, in turn, will nurture wealth and power.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: The Montelli family is set to break the Order's suffocating hold and lead Ragunna towards a wider horizon—embracing Septimont, Huanglong, and other countries. And through it all, we will never forget who we are.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: The family exists to accomplish what one cannot and withstand the winds of fate. This is the future I envision for the Montellis.

options.text: What do you plan to do next?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Like I said, we seize control.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: An all-out war with the Order isn't a wise move. Rather than blindly tailing Capollo in search of a hidden data module, let's take control and have him come to us.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Scarlet Velvet mentioned they need a formal reply, and we can deliver that.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: {PlayerName}, care to help me give them an unforgettable response in two days?

options.text: We're taking the initiative. I like it.
Have him come to us... That makes things interesting.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: The rain's back. I remember telling Grandfather how I hated the rainy season.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: I once saw rain as a bad omen in Ragunna.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: When it rains, it's easier to wash away blood stains. That's why acts of revenge usually happen on such days.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: But today's rain feels different. As it falls, the troubles of yesterday are washed away. I feel calm, like I've found my true self again. My present and my future are now in my own hands.

items.speaker: Francesco
items.text: Well done, Carlotta. And thank you, {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Francesco
items.text: My girl could not have accomplished this without your assistance.

options.text: She personally invited and vouched for me, after all.
She is capable enough in her own right.

items.speaker: Francesco
items.text: I had faith in you two, but you surpassed my expectations.

items.speaker: Francesco
items.text: It appears I underestimated the potential that you possess when working together.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: What he just said... Looks like someone has been keeping secrets from me, huh?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: I've been curious, you entered that Sonoro and delivered his gun to me. How did you earn my grandfather's trust so soon?

options.text: That's a story between the two of us.
I'll tell you next time.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Hm, the nerve... Fine, since we make such a good team, I'll let it slide for now.

items.speaker: Francesco
items.text: Capollo and his lackeys will face the consequences of their actions. Our code is simple—vengeance for blood, death for life. No one is exempt.

items.speaker: Francesco
items.text: There's a lot at play here. It will require some effort and a true Montelli at my side to handle it.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: I see. I'll take care of the rest.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: But before that...

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Yes. What, miss me already?

options.text: Of course not.
Yes, I'll miss you. Who knows when I'll see you again.
Well... maybe a little.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: "No need to feel sad about parting, for each goodbye brings us closer to the next hello."

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: That's my favorite way to think of it—a countdown to our next reunion.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: With Capollo ousted, the family needs someone to fill his shoes. I must assist my grandfather through this tumultuous transition. There is no one else to do it.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Thank you, {PlayerName}. As my grandfather said, things couldn't have gone so smoothly without your help and support.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Now that the dust has settled, you no longer need to stay with me as Cat's Eye of the Montelli family, but still... I want you to have this.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: As I said, every member gets a token that matches their codename.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Though you already have a pair of Cat's Eyes, please accept this as a token of my appreciation. No, this gem wasn't crafted by my Forte. I chose it just for you.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: I know someone like you won't stay just for me, and joining the Montelli family might not be in your plans just yet.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: But I'll keep this codename for you, Cat's Eye—the first and only member to receive my personal invitation.
In the meantime, Carlotta is seeing fragments of her past in a mysterious space.

items.speaker: Frightened Child
items.text: W-what is that...

items.speaker: Dignified Voice
items.text: The family will shield you

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: "The midnight chime has faded, yet the night endures. Restless hearts long for an understanding that should be ours."

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: "In the freest place of Ragunna, I await your arrival..." This should be the place Capollo mentioned in his invitation.

items.speaker: Invitation Letter
items.text: "With this invitation, I wish to personally thank you for all you have done for the Montelli family and to amend the slight misstep in your reception. I hope this gesture will help to mend any misunderstandings and strengthen our alliance."

items.speaker: Invitation Letter
items.text: "In the name of the Montellis, I shall honor my allies as I do my own kin and blood."

items.speaker: Invitation Letter
items.text: "In the freest place of Ragunna, I await your arrival... Yours sincerely, Capollo Montelli."

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: I knew it, another invitation. That's kind of their signature, huh? But this wasn't Carlotta, who's this <color=yellow>Capollo</color> guy?

options.text: He's the uncle Carlotta told us about.
He's the owner of that pendant.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Oh, yeah. The big boss of the vault and all that jazz? Guess it won't hurt swinging by Carlotta's family. They do owe us a big one!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: I remember now, didn't he get nabbed by the Order?

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Then he's got a lot to thank you for. When that sneaky Priest tried to use him as a pawn, things could've gotten messy without you and Carlotta's quick thinking.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: I bet he's got something good planned. A pile of money? Maybe a mega feast?

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Ugh... hic! I've been feeling pretty weird lately, my head's all wobbly. I might not be able to show right when you need me!

options.text: Just get some rest, I can manage.
I'll make sure to eat all the food for you.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Okay, you've got this. If anything happens, just smash your way through!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Alright, alright! No need to rub it in!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Anyway, I'll leave this to you then. But hey, if you ever need my help, just give me a shout, okay?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Hm, to locate me so precisely and deliver the invitation right into my hands... this Capollo Montelli knows what he's doing.
Forgive me for not meeting you properly until now.
Guest from the Black Shores, Laureate of the Carnevale, savior and friend of the Montellis... {PlayerName}, your presence is an honor.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: Allow me to formally introduce myself. I am Capollo Montelli, Carlotta's uncle, <color=yellow>son of the Padrino</color>, and <color=yellow>the next head of the family</color>.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (Given Capollo's stature, a private meeting at this hour... is it really just about gratitude?)

options.text: Why the sudden invite from someone of your rank?
I hope this meeting is as simple as it seems.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: Of course, just as stated in the invitation. I'm a man of my word.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: Without you, the traitors wouldn't have been captured, and I freed from their hands.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: While Carlotta has expressed our family's gratitude, I feel obliged to do it in person.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: But unfortunately, the traitors had little to offer, and the one behind them is still in the shadows. Unnerving, isn't it?

options.text: Don't worry, Carlotta is on it.
I heard it's someone high up in the family.
Sorry, your family issues aren't my concern.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: I'll wait for her findings then.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: Hmm... that does narrow down the possibilities.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: Be as involved as you like, {PlayerName}. I won't think any less of you.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: Things aren't as bright for the Montellis as they may seem. The Order, Fisalias, and the Fractsidus... Any attempt to break the status quo puts us at risk.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: The traitors we captured were a warning, but also a reminder—to survive, I must seek alliances beyond the family.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: Especially with you, just as Carlotta did.

options.text: You or Carlotta, what's the difference?
Thought I was already an ally to the entire family.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: Now that you mention it, let me be clear.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: Carlotta is dear to me, and nothing will change that.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: But she's from the lesser branch of the family. Tradition says I, the rightful heir, should manage dealings with the Black Shores, not her.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: Restoring order and benefiting the family as I intended... that's what I meant in the letter by amending a slight misstep.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: The leader of the Black Shores must have sent you here with a purpose. Tell me, and I will offer you support far beyond what Carlotta could provide.

options.text: I can't give you an answer now.
Support with a high price, I suppose.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: The choice is yours, of course... But as the family heir, I will not see my authority undermined, even by those I consider allies.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: So if you can't stand by my side, I'd prefer you stay out of the Montellis' affairs.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: Otherwise... believe me, you won't want to find yourself at odds with us, no matter how strong you are.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: You're welcome to remain an observer from the sidelines. But once you join the table, you play by the rules—my rules.
Well said. But the rules are not set in stone, are they?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: What matter of intrigue brought you and {PlayerName} together at this hour, Uncle? A table... are we speaking of a splendid card game, then?

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: Your timing is most unfortunate. Our little chat just finished.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Pity. I didn't think you'd pull {Male=him;Female=her} aside for a private word, especially after all Rover and I have been through together.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: But since you've finished, would you excuse us now?

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: Carlotta, you stay. I have more to discuss with you.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Meet me at the <color=yellow>rendezvous</color>. Beware the "eyes."

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: A gun is a powerful tool, but only in the hands of the worthy.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: I understand, Uncle. I've never forgotten where my privileges come from, nor will I covet what's not mine.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: The Black Shores, the Carnevale... my sole principle is whether they benefit the Montellis.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: I will execute any command that strengthens the family, and crush any threats to our standing.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: ...Very well. Keep your words in mind.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Uncle, about Benett... have you heard?

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: That family thief stealing from the vault? Why, are you suggesting I failed my duties?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Not at all, I just happened to overhear someone talking about it in passing...

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Someone extracted the core data module... It's empty now.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: This must be what Benett was trying to tell us before he died. Someone with access to the vault has broken their oath and leaked the data module. Now, only Uncle, Grandfather, and I have the clearance to retrieve it.

options.text: So that clears up all the questions.
I see. But what can he gain from it?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: In Rinascita, the Order maintains control over Terminals, unlike other countries where they are freely traded. My grandfather learned to modify them during his early ventures in the New Federation.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Our lantern-type Terminal is a modified model. It may look purely ornamental, but it houses the tech modules we've developed. As Scarlet Velvet said, aside from our art dealings, we've been supplying these Terminals to Resonators at fair prices as part of our discreet business.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: By doing so, we've broken the Order's monopoly. We've given more people the means to protect themselves in their ventures, giving them choices they didn't have before.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: The Order is aware of our discreet business, but they turn a blind eye as long as we don't openly challenge their authority. The hefty taxes we pay maintain this... delicate understanding.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: As a result, no family member would ever divulge our Terminal secrets, much less allow the modules to fall into the wrong hands. What exactly are you planning, Uncle?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: No, we must find the core module...

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: Carlotta, where are you?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Uncle, may I ask you something first? Before the Carnevale, the Priest showed me your pendant. How did he come by it, and what led to your capture?

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: Does it matter? All you need to know is that it's handled, and I'm in the clear now.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Were you mistreated? Did they coerce you into doing something against your will?

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: Heed my words. Whatever you've uncovered, put it behind you, and all will be as it was. No grudges, no consequences, only family.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: Now, we've had some trouble with a shipment over at Septimont. As the rightful heir, I'm giving you a new order. Take care of it, and I'll pretend none of this ever happened.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: I refuse.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: Excuse me?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: I said I refuse! I can't act as if nothing happened. I won't let a family member die unjustly, or see our modules fall into the wrong hands.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: Don't trouble yourself with the module. Yes, our family's ambition has led us far, but it's exacted a price paid in blood and tears. What harm is there in choosing the safer, easier path?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: I can't believe you just said that. So, you're refusing to tell the truth and reveal the module's location, is that it?

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: Why do you insist on tearing everything apart? Have you ever thought about what Padrino would say? What others will think of us? I've given you an out, all you need to do is accept it.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Then so be it.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Your course is set. As is mine. But mark my words... I will stop you, Mr. Capollo.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: I'm sorry you have to see this side of our family.

options.text: It's okay to be a little less perfect with friends.
It's messy, but still manageable.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Really? I just wish... No, I did promise I'd try to drop the pretense with you.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: I may need more time to come to terms with all this. I don't regret uncovering the truth and exposing the traitor… I'm just saddened that my uncle is the one behind it all.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: We were meant to be the guardians of our family's ideals, to carry the torch forward. And yet, now…

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Well, wallowing in emotion won't solve anything. Our priority now is to recover the data module. Every moment it's missing puts our family at greater risk.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: The core module is difficult to carry around. They must have hidden it... The dock Benett visited, used for storage... Could it be there?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: We need to adjust our strategy. My conversation with Capollo will have alerted him. He's likely to move the module sooner than expected. I need to inspect the dock for any clues.

options.text: We may restore more data in this Terminal.
There could still be other possibilities.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Indeed, but time is not on our side. If Capollo acts on this, I might lose my window to secure the module. Our sole advantage now is that we're operating as a pair.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: I need you to take this terminal to <color=yellow>Pierre</color> at the bank. If the dock turns out to be a dead end, we will need other leads.

options.text: What's your next move if we find the module?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: I know that securing the module alone won't suffice. I must uncover Capollo's true motives...how it will affect our family. I'll figure it out, I promise.
According to the plan, you and Carlotta part ways. She heads alone to the dock she mentioned.

items.speaker: Old Gentleman
items.text: Hello again.

options.text: Hello again, Carlotta's grandfather.
You must be Francesco, the current head of the Montelli family.
Should I call you Padrino like the rest do?

items.speaker: Francesco
items.text: It would be my honor if you do.

items.speaker: Francesco
items.text: You don't seem very surprised to learn who I am.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Carlotta told me Mr. Pierre always follows her grandfather around. My instincts weren't wrong when I saw you together.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: It also explains why you seemed familiar when we first met.

options.text: Was our previous meeting really a coincidence?

items.speaker: Francesco
items.text: What's the term for it... A destined encounter? The universe brought us together, and I happened to take the initiative. It's almost poetic, wouldn't you agree?

items.speaker: Francesco
items.text: That's it, I doubt you want more chit-chat. I know you're here for that Terminal.

items.speaker: Francesco
items.text: Let Pierre inspect it for you and we can resume our conversation.

items.speaker: Francesco
items.text: Remember that painting we discussed earlier?

items.speaker: Francesco
items.text: In a fragile equilibrium, the smaller piece is forced to repress its true nature. It's confined to a seemingly flawless structure, stripped of its autonomy. I don't want the same fate for her.

items.speaker: Francesco
items.text: But I can't simply impose my path upon her, expecting blind obedience. She must find her own path, with a partner she can fully trust.

items.speaker: Francesco
items.text: As for those who betray their own blood, there will be no leniency. Even my own son is not exempt from this.

items.speaker: Francesco
items.text: However, before I intervene, I want to see her plan take shape. My hand should only be played as a final measure.

items.speaker: Francesco
items.text: The old give way to the new, that is the way of the world. But for us Montellis, the courage to break from the past matters far more than clinging to it.

items.speaker: Francesco
items.text: So, I ask you this favor. When the time is right, give this to her. She will need it.

items.speaker: Pierre
items.text: It's a little tricky... but you're lucky to have my expertise!

items.speaker: Pierre
items.text: This Terminal is defunct, but I've retrieved all its data, even the overwritten and isolated bits.

options.text: You mean, data was erased from it?

items.speaker: Pierre
items.text: The Terminal has been tampered with, but nothing was intentionally erased. It seems they wanted to overwrite the data to avoid detection.

items.speaker: Pierre
items.text: I can show you. Do you have a Terminal? I'll send over the data so you can see for yourself.

options.text: These records...

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: I assume you've taken care of Benett's body?

items.speaker: Capollo's Agent
items.text: We... dumped him in the Tacet Discord zone overnight. We'll claim he was killed by those monsters tomorrow morning.

items.speaker: Capollo's Agent
items.text: Sir... are you sure? Are we really going to work with the Order... after they made you kill one of our own?

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: We're in too deep now. Handing over the data module to the Order will cement out position as an ally of the authorities. We'll replace the Fisalias! The other Resonators in the city are irrelevant.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: And Benett... I would have taken care of him regardless.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: He knew too much. He deserved it for pursuing the Scarlet Velvet matter. Anyone who gets in my way shall meet the same fate.

items.speaker: Capollo's Agent
items.text: Y-Yes, sir...

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: We all stood together on that stormy night, each one of us losing someone dear. And we all share the desire for our family to find an easier path forward.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: The best way to avoid sacrifice isn't fighting, but aligning ourselves with superior powers and knowing when to yield.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: You may have left the family, and I should expect no more favors from you. But I ask this of you one last time. Please, put this Terminal in Carlotta's hands.

items.speaker: Duncan
items.text: What's in it? Carlotta.. What will happen to her?

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: Nothing, save for the standard location tracking data every Terminal holds.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: In three days, I'll be negotiating on behalf of the family with the Order. I just want to make sure Carlotta doesn't interfere in the meantime. She'll be given a chance to join our side, I promise.

items.speaker: Capollo
items.text: Think about Benett. If you take care of this, I will overlook what he stole and let it be a thing of the past. I know you care more about your late friend than you're willing to admit.

items.speaker: Capollo's Agent
items.text: Unbelievable. What is Capollo even thinking? Killing one of our own... It's a complete betrayal of our family! I must record all this madness with something...

items.speaker: Capollo's Agent
items.text: My Terminal? No, it's too risky. Wait... I have Benett's body... Nobody's going to pay attention to a dead man's Terminal.

items.speaker: Capollo's Agent
items.text: I'll record it, then overwrite with new data.... I have to keep this record well... In case I also...

options.text: That Terminal... It was part of Capollo's schemes!
They set a trap in the docks...

items.speaker: Pierre
items.text: A trap? Wait, sir... Wouldn't Lady Carlotta be in danger? I don't think that is part of your plan. Are you really letting her face this by herself?

items.speaker: Francesco
items.text: She's already there, so why bother? I trust Carlotta and our esteemed guest. It is a trap, but also an opportunity for her to mature.

items.speaker: Francesco
items.text: She's already there, so why bother? I trust Carlotta and our esteemed guest. It is a trap, but also an opportunity for her to mature.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: The rain's back. I remember telling Grandfather how I hated the rainy season.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: I once saw rain as a bad omen in Ragunna.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: When it rains, it's easier to wash away blood stains. That's why acts of revenge usually happen on such days.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: But today's rain feels different. As it falls, the troubles of yesterday are washed away. I feel calm, like I've found my true self again. My present and my future are now in my own hands.

items.speaker: Francesco
items.text: Well done, Carlotta. And thank you, {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Francesco
items.text: My girl could not have accomplished this without your assistance.

options.text: She personally invited and vouched for me, after all.
She is capable enough in her own right.

items.speaker: Francesco
items.text: I had faith in you two, but you surpassed my expectations.

items.speaker: Francesco
items.text: It appears I underestimated the potential that you possess when working together.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: What he just said... Looks like someone has been keeping secrets from me, huh?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: I've been curious, you entered that Sonoro and delivered his gun to me. How did you earn my grandfather's trust so soon?

options.text: That's a story between the two of us.
I'll tell you next time.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Hm, the nerve... Fine, since we make such a good team, I'll let it slide for now.

items.speaker: Francesco
items.text: Capollo and his lackeys will face the consequences of their actions. Our code is simple—vengeance for blood, death for life. No one is exempt.

items.speaker: Francesco
items.text: There's a lot at play here. It will require some effort and a true Montelli at my side to handle it.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: I see. I'll take care of the rest.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: But before that...

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Yes. What, miss me already?

options.text: Of course not.
Yes, I'll miss you. Who knows when I'll see you again.
Well... maybe a little.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: "No need to feel sad about parting, for each goodbye brings us closer to the next hello."

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: That's my favorite way to think of it—a countdown to our next reunion.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: With Capollo ousted, the family needs someone to fill his shoes. I must assist my grandfather through this tumultuous transition. There is no one else to do it.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Thank you, {PlayerName}. As my grandfather said, things couldn't have gone so smoothly without your help and support.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Now that the dust has settled, you no longer need to stay with me as Cat's Eye of the Montelli family, but still... I want you to have this.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: As I said, every member gets a token that matches their codename.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Though you already have a pair of Cat's Eyes, please accept this as a token of my appreciation. No, this gem wasn't crafted by my Forte. I chose it just for you.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: I know someone like you won't stay just for me, and joining the Montelli family might not be in your plans just yet.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: But I'll keep this codename for you, Cat's Eye—the first and only member to receive my personal invitation.
